context: Defines validation rules for AODS corpus integrity. AODS v3 adds surface-pair, generated-human-surface, claim-conflict, layer, capsule, touch-route, artifact-semantic, control-taxonomy, provenance-summary, runtime-contract, surface-reality, and experimental bidirectional-sync checks on top of schema, referential integrity, completeness, and AOP compliance.
assumptions: Validator has access to all files in corpus
JSON Schema draft-07 validation is available

sections.content: L1 MUST: manifest.json exists and validates against manifest.schema.json. Every modules[].path resolves inside corpus root. Every module file validates against module.schema.json. module_id matches manifest entry. No duplicate module IDs. No duplicate sid within module. Module schema permits section-first, artifact-first, or mixed modules, but every module must declare at least one section or artifact. boot_sequence and boot_by_role contain valid module IDs. boot_by_touch.match stays relative and corpus-contained. surface_pairs records match schema and keep human paths corpus-contained. human_generation, when declared, stays within the allowed opt-in contract: mode=deterministic, profile=overview|checklist, title optional. manifest-companion roles are runtime role profiles: id required; scope may be omitted; required_modules may be omitted when boot_by_role already carries runtime bindings. control metadata, provenance summaries, and runtime-contract summaries, when declared, validate on both manifest.modules[] and module.meta. surface-inventory entries keep relative paths and typed current|reserved|future states.

L2 MUST: deps[] resolve to manifest.modules[].id. section.refs[] resolve. section.artifact_refs[] resolve. role.required_modules[] resolve when declared. meta.capsule.routes_to resolve. boot_by_touch.load_modules[] resolve. surface_pairs.agent_primary and agent_supporting resolve. human_primary and human_supporting paths exist inside corpus root. Generated human_primary must re-render exactly from the declared deterministic generation contract. Paired human and agent surfaces must not disagree on conservatively extractable high-signal claims such as from-to deltas, must-include lists, timed actions, and execution preconditions. Claim-diff extraction reads agent_primary context, sections, and structured artifact semantics, and it compares full claim sets rather than treating one overlapping value as agreement. Artifact-internal references also resolve: process-flow next targets, subprocess_ref, payload_schema, decision-tree node targets, topology endpoints, group node IDs, and mapping-table row width against declared columns. surface-inventory declarations keep relative paths even without reality mode. Under validate --reality, current entries resolve from the selected base: base=corpus -> corpus root; base=repo -> --repo-root when supplied, else corpus root. Reality mode rejects duplicate current declarations and distinguishes missing current paths from reserved or future declarations. Circular module deps forbidden; circular section refs allowed with warning.

sections.topic: surface pair layer and capsule checks
sections.content: L3 SHOULD: every human-facing primary surface has paired agent_primary when corpus declares surface_pairs. boot_sequence starts with layer=root modules; capsule modules may follow root modules; detail and evidence should not lead boot. category=capsule <-> layer=capsule. category=capsule -> meta.capsule.{read_when, core_question, routes_to} present. sync_source=bidirectional warns until merge protocol exists, and the reference hook requires manual review when bidirectional pairs change. boot_by_touch should stay specific enough to narrow authoring loads instead of recreating full boot_by_role lists. Hook layer rejects paired human-surface edits when sync_source=agent-primary and no paired agent module changed in the same change set, unless the changed human file is a valid deterministic regeneration; hook also rejects agent-only edits when sync_source=human-primary. manifest-only metadata edits do not satisfy semantic sync. shared_invariants, when declared on a surface_pair, must remain present in both agent_primary and human_primary texts. Claim-diff checks stay conservative and only fire when validator can deterministically align both sides. If control metadata is declared, manifest.modules[].control and module.meta.control should match exactly, and sensor-only modules should not live in root or capsule layers. If provenance metadata is declared, manifest.modules[].provenance and module.meta.provenance should agree on memory_role + confidence. If runtime contracts are declared, manifest.modules[].runtime_contract and module.meta.runtime_contract should agree on side_effects + outputs, and side effects beyond none should expose telemetry, checkpoints, or outputs. surface-inventory reality checks are opt-in through validate --reality so existing corpora do not silently change behavior. base=repo only needs --repo-root when repo-scoped paths must resolve outside the corpus root. Module-like JSON files discovered under declared module directories should be registered in manifest.modules[]; otherwise validator warns that they are disk-only and unloaded. tokens_approx populated for all modules. Stable or frozen modules contain no TODO: or UNDEFINED: markers.

L2 MUST: if manifest.modules[].provenance or module.meta.provenance is declared, both sides stay present and memory_role + confidence match. L2 MUST: if manifest.modules[].runtime_contract or module.meta.runtime_contract is declared, both sides stay present and side_effects + outputs match. L2 MUST: runtime contracts with side effects other than none expose at least one observable anchor via telemetry, checkpoints, or outputs. L2 MUST: when surface-inventory artifacts run under validate --reality, current directories contain non-placeholder material and current file|directory kinds match the declared kind. These rules keep bootstrap routing aligned with deeper execution and prevent invisible side effects or stale current-state claims.

L4 SHOULD: no markdown syntax in content fields. No filler phrases. No conversational tone. No positional refs. Regex-based checks should ignore quoted anti-pattern examples when spec modules cite forbidden strings literally. Token density score > 3.5. No section content exceeds 2000 tokens. Capsule summaries stay shorter than detail modules they route to.

Validator outputs JSON: {corpus:str, timestamp:iso8601, levels:{L1,L2,L3,L4}, summary:{total_modules:int, total_sections:int, total_artifacts:int, total_tokens:int, total_surface_pairs:int, errors:int, warnings:int}}. issue = {level:str, module_id:str|null, sid:str|null, rule:str, message:str}.

artifacts.usage: Lookup table for validation level semantics.

rows: missing manifest; invalid module schema; invalid surface_pairs shape; invalid human_generation enum

broken refs; broken routes_to; missing human path; generated human surface drift; paired claim conflict; control metadata mismatch; provenance summary mismatch; runtime contract mismatch; invisible side effects; sensor declared in root/capsule; invalid process-flow targets; invalid topology endpoints; mapping-table row mismatch; missing or placeholder-only current surfaces in reality mode
capsule missing meta.capsule; boot order starts with detail; missing tokens_approx; unregistered module-like JSON on disk
markdown in content; filler prose; oversized section

artifacts.usage: Surface-pair, layer, and capsule validation rules.

rules.action: L1 error: agent_primary missing
L1 error: human_primary must stay inside corpus root
L1 error: boot_by_touch match must stay inside corpus root
L2 error: capsule metadata missing
L2 error: capsule routes_to unresolved
L2 error: declared shared_invariants missing from paired surfaces
L2 error: paired surfaces contain contradictory high-signal claims
L2 error: generated human surfaces require agent-primary sync
L2 error: generated human surface is out of sync with deterministic render

rules.condition: module.control.declared == true AND mirror_match(module.control, manifest.modules[].control) == false
rules.action: L2 error: manifest.modules[].control must match module.meta.control

rules.condition: module.provenance.declared == true AND mirror_match(module.provenance.summary, manifest.modules[].provenance) == false
rules.action: L2 error: manifest.modules[].provenance must match module.meta.provenance summary fields
L2 error: manifest.modules[].runtime_contract must match module.meta.runtime_contract summary fields
L2 error: runtime contracts with side effects must declare telemetry, checkpoints, or outputs
L2 error: current surfaces must exist in the selected corpus or repo root
L2 error: current directories must contain non-placeholder material
L2 error: current surfaces must not be declared twice for the same base/path/kind
L2 error: sensor-only modules must not live in root or capsule layers
L3 warning: boot should start with root layer
L2 error: boot_by_touch load_modules unresolved
L3 warning: evidence should not participate in initial boot
L3 warning: bidirectional sync remains experimental until merge protocol exists

changelog.delta: Added surface-pair, layer, capsule, and boot-order validation rules plus total_surface_pairs in validation output.
Clarified capsule layer/category identity and added explicit bidirectional-sync warning semantics.
Hardened corpus-contained path rules and clarified that manifest-only edits do not satisfy agent-primary hook sync.
Added shared_invariants surface-pair validation so declared semantic anchors become built-in detectable drift failures.
Added conservative paired-surface claim-conflict validation for deterministic high-signal semantic contradictions.
Added control metadata mirroring and sensor-layer restrictions for the explicit control taxonomy.
Added provenance summary mirroring and runtime-contract observability rules so bootstrap metadata stays aligned with deeper execution contracts.
Added opt-in surface-inventory reality validation for current path existence, kind checks, duplicate current declarations, and placeholder-only directory detection.
